planTitle: Work-document format redesign: spec, plan, architecture + behavior spine + gate contracts

content: Canonical work-artifact format rules live in `domains/shared/skills/work-artifacts/` as a thin `SKILL.md` dispatcher with directly linked references; role skills such as `/skill:plan`, `/skill:task`, and `/skill:tdd` should route to that shared skill instead of duplicating spec/plan/architecture, behavior-spine, or gate-contract rules.

Use four workflow tiers when choosing artifact ceremony: direct fixes use TDD and a regression test only; tactical bugfix/task work may use a small task or tiny plan without a spec; planned feature/refactor work uses `spec.md` plus a behavior-first `plan.md`; architectural or multi-plan work adds a durable architecture record.

For planned work, specs use stable `AC-###` IDs and plans decompose them into `B-###` behavior entries with context, action, expected result, source AC, seam, named test, and a plain `@cosmo-behavior plan:<slug>#B-###` marker placed near the executable test; a behavior with no named test or marker means the plan is not ready.

Durable architecture records belong at `missions/architecture/<slug>.md`, not inside implementation plan directories or `memory/`; create them only when they change implementation or review through durable boundaries, dependency rules, state ownership, or multi-plan decisions, and have dependent plans cite them in an `Architecture Context` section.

Generic `Quality Contract` sections are ordered gate ladders by gate kind, tier, binding state, threshold, and degradation notes; generic artifacts must not contain concrete tool names or command columns, because project-specific bindings and gate execution protocols are deferred to follow-up enforcement/configuration work.

`quality-manager` must parse abstract gate-ladder tables separately from legacy `QC-*` criteria: rows with `Gate kind`, `Tier`, and `Binding state` become `gate_ladder_rows`, unbound bindable gates are reported in `degraded_gates`, bound rows without protocol are `protocol pending`, and ladder rows must not be warned or skipped as malformed legacy criteria merely because they lack `QC-*`, `verification`, or `command` fields.

Agents with explicit skill allowlists do not automatically see new shared skills: because skill assembly filters explicit agents to their declared list, `planner`, `spec-writer`, `task-manager`, and especially `plan-reviewer` had to add `work-artifacts` (and relevant `architecture`/`task`) explicitly or they could not load the artifact contract they are expected to produce or review.

Review and verification prompts should consume artifact guidance only when artifact conformance or plan context is in scope: reviewers load `work-artifacts` for behavior markers, architecture context, and gate-ladder claims only when relevant; verifiers validate explicit artifact-conformance claims with evidence and must not expand beyond the provided claims; integration-verifier audits declared architecture context, boundary models, behavior seams, and abstract gate rows without inventing unstated rules.

Task decomposition for behavior-first plans must preserve behavior ownership: task acceptance criteria name the owned `B-###` behaviors, carry the expected `@cosmo-behavior` marker into worker context, assign every behavior cluster to at least one task, and avoid making workers invent missing architecture or artifact structure.

For prompt and skill contract tests, assert stable contract phrases, behavior IDs, marker syntax, routing instructions, and refusal rules instead of snapshotting full markdown files; include mutation-style negative checks such as no full-stack ceremony for direct fixes, no concrete gate tools in generic references, no shelfware architecture records, and no silent skipping of abstract gate-ladder rows.
